audit_items.proof_trace: The sole deallocation is `delete[] data` at the `done:` label (L130), immediately followed by `return result` with no further use of `data`. All reader objects that hold a copy of the `data` pointer are stack-local within `read_binary_objdump` switch-case blocks and are fully destroyed before any of those calls return, so no live reference to `data` exists when `delete[]` executes. `ObjdumpState` accumulates only `std::string` copies and integer relocation records — no raw pointers into the file buffer — and no global stores a pointer to the per-file allocation.
